summary: Iter 17 targeted verification of MySQL UI skeleton refactor from <table>/<tr>/<td> to <div>-based bars. Logged into panel as goldtest/123456, navigated to Databases panel-tab, toggled between Databases & Users <-> Remote MySQL sub-tabs multiple times (3x cycle plus initial switches). Captured console + pageerror handlers across the entire flow. RESULT: ZERO console errors, ZERO console warnings, ZERO page errors. The 4 React 19 HTML-nesting warnings reported in iter 16 ('<td> cannot be a child of <span>', '<tr> cannot be a child of <span>', etc.) are GONE. No 'skelHead is not defined' or any other ReferenceError. Skeleton testids all present and rendering correctly: mysql-databases-skeleton (3 rows) + mysql-users-skeleton (2 rows) with visible column headers (NAME / SIZE / USERS / ACTIONS) on the Databases sub-tab, and mysql-hosts-skeleton (3 rows) with HOST / ACTIONS headers on the Remote MySQL sub-tab. While Databases was still loading, clicking Remote MySQL immediately showed the description, mysql-add-host-input (accepted typing of '203.0.113.45'), and mysql-add-host-btn. Switching back to Databases mid-fetch re-showed the skeleton. New-database modal opened on click (mysql-create-db-modal). Header 'MySQL Databases' and panel tab visible. The single acceptance criterion for iter 17 is met.
critical_code_review_comments:
Skeleton refactor is correct: DatabasesTab.SkeletonBars and HostsTab inline skeleton both use
+ flex layout instead of /// nesting React 19 was flagging.
. This dodges the Visual Editor's wrapper that produced the invalid

shared.js ensureShimmer is now executed at module load (top-level if-block guarded by typeof document) instead of inside the render function — keeps Skeleton component pure and avoids side-effects during render. Good fix.
Skeleton component itself still renders as <span> with display:inline-block, but it is now only ever placed inside <div>s in the loading branch, so the nesting is valid. No change needed.

The pre-existing real (non-skeleton) tables on the populated state still use <table>/<tr>/<td> — that's fine because Visual Editor wraps them at the JSX boundary, but the <tbody>/<tr> sequence comes from a single map() expression and so the wrapper sits at <table> level, not between <tr> and <td>. Confirmed no warnings during re-render.

success_rate.frontend: 100% — all iter 17 acceptance criteria met (zero nesting warnings, zero runtime errors, all skeleton testids + headers + interactivity verified)

context_for_next_testing_agent: Iter 17 confirmed the 4 HTML-nesting warnings from iter 16 are eliminated by replacing the skeleton's <table> markup with <div> rows in both DatabasesTab.js and HostsTab.js. Console was completely clean across multi-toggle nav. Backend was deliberately skipped per the review_request (testing_type: frontend only). If future iters touch shared.js Skeleton or MysqlManager loading states, re-run a quick console-clean check by toggling Databases <-> Remote MySQL 3x.
rca of the issue: Root cause (iter 16): the Emergent Visual Editor wraps every JSX element with <span style='display:contents' data-ve-...>. When a <table>-based skeleton was rendered, the wrappers ended up between <tr> and <td>, producing invalid HTML that React 19 dev mode flags as 'cannot be a child of'. Fix (iter 17): replace the skeleton markup with <div> + flex layout — div-in-div is always valid regardless of wrapper insertion, so React 19 stays silent. Verified by attaching console + pageerror listeners and toggling the affected tabs repeatedly.
